Chapter ten: Of The Taste For Physical well-being In America
In America the passion for physical well-being is not always exclusive, but it is general; and if all do not feel it in the same manner, yet it is felt by all.
The comforts of life are not to them the end of life, but simply a way of living; they regard them as existence itself-enjoyed, but scarcely thought of.

Most of these wealthy persons were once poor; they have felt the sting of want; they were long a prey to adverse fortunes; and now that the victory is won, the passions which accompanied the contest have survived it: their minds are, as it were, intoxicated by the small enjoyments which they have pursued for forty years.
These are small objects, but the soul clings to them; it dwells upon them closely and day by day, till they at last shut out the rest of the world, and sometimes intervene between itself and heaven.

I contest the proposition: in point of physical gratifications, the most opulent members of a democracy will not display tastes very different from those of the people; whether it be that, springing from the people, they really share those tastes, or that they esteem it a duty to submit to them.

Therefore it follows that the essence of the soul is not its power.

For the substance is all that belongs to the essence of a thing; whereas whatever is beyond the essence of a thing cannot be called accident in this sense; but only what is not caused by the essential principle of the species.

But the power of the soul does not regard the nature of the contrary as such, but rather the common aspect of both contraries; as sight does not regard white as such, but as color.
For in those things which come under one division, there is no before and after, but all are naturally simultaneous.
But the action of one power of the soul does not depend on that of another; for sight can act independently of hearing, and conversely.
But some operations of the soul are performed by means of corporeal organs; as sight by the eye, and hearing by the ear.
Now the powers of the soul are oppositely divided, as various species. Therefore one of them does not proceed from another.
